# Learning JavaScript #

This project describes my joruney of learning JS using the metaphor of building a computer. Each section highlights 
a different element of JS that I have learned, and how it applies to building a computer. 
The index holds 3-4 groups that are utilized through scrolling. One holds the main elements on the left and keeps them sticky while I animate over them.
The second is the text content that scrolls on the right.
I use CSS variables a main styles file, and a normalize file. I have 2 JS files, the main houses the functionality of the theme toggle, and the animations file 
holds all of the animation and gsap elements.

## Tech Stack ##
HTML
CSS
JS
GSAP
ScrollTrigger
DrawSVG

## Links ##
https://www.mothcentral.com/scrollytelling-LearningJavascript-LandonU/ - Live Site


## Reflection ##
The metaphor is building a Pc and how some of the steps are similar to what I have done with JS.

There isn't a particular section I enjoy or am proud of I have grown to loathe this project.

I have had a lot of hell with bugs and technical issues such as elements not properly lining up between browsers and monitors. 
I fixed the main ones that were site breaking, but the lining up I did not fix. 

The only accessibilty setting I made was adding the reduced motion into my CSS file. 

With more time I would completely do another thing entirely. My idea became the largest pain in the ass since starting college.
